title: Cannot disable cgroups with docker-compose
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The 'cgroups' configuration setting in **/etc/containers/containers.conf** is not used by the podman system service when containers are started with docker-compose. I need to be able to disable cgroups for a collection of containers started with docker-compose.

In my **/etc/containers/containers.conf**, I have this line under the [containers] table:
`cgroups = "disabled"`

When I run a container, the system setting takes affect and my container shows cgroups as disabled:

```
root@device:~# podman container inspect wizardly_rosalind | grep -i cgroup
"--cgroup-manager",
"CgroupManager": "disabled",
"CgroupMode": "private",
"Cgroup": "",
"Cgroups": "disabled",
"CgroupParent": "",
"CgroupConf": null
```

However, when I start the same container with docker-compose, the container starts but it has cgroups enabled.

```
root@device:~# podman container inspect data_collector  | grep Cgroup
"CgroupManager": "systemd",
"CgroupMode": "private",
"Cgroup": "",
"Cgroups": "default",
"CgroupParent": "",
"CgroupConf": null
```

I cannot set 'cgroups: disabled' in docker-compose.yml, it is not a recognized configuration key. How can I get docker-compose to honor the setting in containers.conf?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set **cgroups = "disabled"** in **/etc/containers/containers.conf**

2. Start any container with docker-compose

3. Inspect the container and note that cgroups are still enabled

**Describe the results you received:**

cgroups are enabled in containers started with docker-compose.

**Describe the results you expected:**

cgroups would be disabled in containers started with docker-compose because the system setting in containers.conf indicates it should.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
root@device:~# podman version
Version:      4.0.0-dev
API Version:  4.0.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.16.5
Built:        Thu Dec  2 15:45:42 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  swapFree: 189530112
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
N/A ( built with Yocto )
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Embedded Linux ARM

comments.body: Is the podman system service expected to honor the containers.conf file? Or is there a different way to disable cgroups for an environment started with docker-compose and podman? Thank you.
I think we expect the Podman APIs to honor containers.conf. I don't think it's as clear with the Docker-compatible APIs.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@umohnani8 Did you ever look into this?
